rawContent: {"stage":"build","insights":[{"kind":"lesson","text":"When auditing auth coverage, the actual code change may be minimal — the bulk of the effort is verification, not modification.","scope":"auth"},{"kind":"gotcha","text":"A leftover 'my-route' placeholder file existed in the codebase and had to be deleted, highlighting that scaffold artifacts should be cleaned up before production.","scope":"src/app"},{"kind":"decision","text":"The /health and /csrf-token endpoints are intentionally left unprotected as they serve infrastructure and CSRF needs respectively.","scope":"auth"},{"kind":"convention","text":"All /api/* routes must use the `withAuth` HOC; exceptions are explicitly enumerated (health, csrf-token) rather than implicit.","scope":"auth"},{"kind":"lesson","text":"Auth audit tasks should first enumerate all routes and then compare against protection status, making deletion the simplest fix when a route shouldn't exist at all.","scope":"auth"}]}
